Sef dist iby 36 etal asso OUT yy

' Recorded by Abu Dawood (#345, 346 and 349), Book on Purity, Chapter on the

gbusl for Friday; al-Tirmidhi (#496), Book on Prayer, Chapter on what has been

narrated concerning the virtues of making ghus/ on Friday. Ibn Khuzaimah (vol. 3, p. 28) and ibn Hibbaan (#559) have declared this hadith sabib.

[It should be noted that this is one (and perhaps not the Strongest) interpretation of this hadith. For a more detailed discussion of the meaning of this hadith, see this translator’s The Friday Prayer Part I: The Figh (Ann Arbor, MI: IANA, 1998, 2 edition), pp. 6465.2]

The Figh of Marriage

“If one of you is gone for a long time, he should not return - unexpectedly! to his wife at night.”? The Prophet (peace be upon him) also said, feat a wg we fk ato te TL of ota ee Hpi AEG i yb Ul BS be SUT a 1,

“Tf one of you returns at night, he should not come to his wife unexpectedly until the one whose husband has been gone can remove her pubic hairs and clean the filth from her hair by combing it.”3

Jaabir also said, “The Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) forbade that a man returns expectedly at night to his wife, finding something that will arouse his suspicion concerning the wives or coming across some of their faults,”

Tbn Hajr explained the above hadith, stating in his commentary,

The one who rushes upon his household [unexpectedly] after a long absence without them realizing it will usually come across some displeasing aspects. He may find his wife unprepared with respect to being clean and beautified in a way that is desired from a woman and that may cause some estrangement between them. The Prophet (peace be upon him) alluded to that aspect when he said, “[until she] can remove her pubic hairs and clean the filth from her hair by combing it.” From that, he may dislike to have contact with her while she is in that state in which

' ‘The phrase used here means to return from a journey at night or at any other time ynexpectedly. See Fath al-Baari, vol. 9, B 340.

Recorded by al-Bukhari (#5244), Book on Marriage, Chapter on not comit unexpectedly to one’s wife at night if one has been gone long, lest he may fin something that will arouse his suspicion concerning the wives or he may come qcross some of their faults.

Recorded by Muslim (#715) [184], Book on Governmental Authority, Chapter on the disapproval of returning unexpectedly at night for the one who returns from a

Rav ded by Muslim (#715) [57], Chapter on Governmental Authority, Chapter on the disapproval of returning unexpectedly at night.

The Figh of Marriage

she has not cleaned herself and so he does not come upon her while she is in a state that will cause him to dislike her. Or he may come across her while she is an unpleasant state. The law tries to conceal people’s faults. The Prophet (peace be upon him) alluded to this aspect when he said, “finding something that will arouse his suspicion concerning the wives or coming across some of their faults.” Based on that, though, if someone informs his wife about when he is going to arrive and that he should be coming at such and such time, this prohibition [of coming at night] does not apply to him.!

The wife must try to make herself appealing to her husband by beautifying herself for him and making herself attractive, in order for him not to shun her. One of the wives of the Prophet (peace be upon him) said to him, “O Messenger of Allah, if a woman does not beautify herself for her husband, she will not be important to him.”? The last part means she will become a burden upon him and he will not like to look at her.

It is authentically reported that Aishah would beautify herself for the Prophet (peace be upoa him), The Prophet (peace be upon him) came upon her once and she had two silver rings. He said, “What is this, O Aishah?” She replied, “I made them in order [to wear them] to beautify myself for you, O Messenger of Allah.”3

| See Fath al-Baari, vol. 9, p. 340.

? Recorded by al-Nasaai bol 9, p. 159), Book on Beautification, Chapter on the disapproval of women Showing their jewelry and gold; Ahmad in a/-Musnad (vol. 2, p. 440), from the hadith of Abu Hurairah. [This particular hadith has been graded weak by both al-Albaani and al-Arnaoot, et al. See Muhammad Naasir al-Deen alAlbaani, Dhaeef Sunan al-Nasaai (Beirut: al-Maktab al-Islaami, 1990), p. 228; Shuaib al-Arnaaot and Aadil Murshid’s footnotes to Ahmad ibn Hanbal, Musnad alImaam {mad ibn Hanbal (Beirut: Muassasah al-Risaalah, 1997), vol. 15, pp. 423-

The Figh of Marriage

Chapter Seven The Rights of Spouses

Upon examination, we find that the rights of the spouses fall into three categories: the rights of the husband, the rights of the wife and common rights of both spouses. Each shall be explained separately below.

The Rights of the Husband upon His Wife

The foundation upon which the husband’s rights over his wife are based is the verse in the Quran,

Lan ih ob iia 35 abi (ead Vay sind oe ols Ne his vay ial obi wat oostass oot \ asl grtatl re Ohl aan Ain sea uy a

“Men are in charge of women by [right of] what [qualities] Allah has given one over the other and what they spend [in support] from their wealth. So righteous women are devoutly obedient, guarding in [the husband’s] absence what Allah would have them guard. But those [women] from whom you fear defiance, [first] admonish them; [then if they persist] forsake. them in their beds and [finally] strike them [lightly]. But if they obey you [once more], seek no means against them” (al-Nisaa 34).

Imam Abu al-Fidaa Ismaeel ibn Katheer stated in his commentary to the words, “Men are in charge of women”:

‘The Fight of Marriage

Men are in charge of women “by [right of] what [qualities] Allah has given one over the other.” In other words, [in general} men are superior to women and men are [in general] better than women. This is why prophethood was only given to men. The same is true for the supreme governmental head, as the Prophet (peace be upon him) said, “A people will not prosper whose affairs are [at the controls of] a woman.” Recorded by alAl-Bukhari! The same is true for judgeship and other Positions. [They are also in charge of women] due to “what they spend [in support] from their wealth,” that is, the dower and maintenance that Allah has made obligatory upon them in His Noble Book and in the sunnah of His Messenger (peace be upon him). Man is better than woman in himself and he has preference over her and superiority. Hence, it is most appropriate that he be in charge of her.?

This does not mean that the same is true for each individual. The superiority of one class of beings over another does not mean that each individual of that class is superior to the other class. Therefore, it is not impossible that some women are greatly superior to some men. Looking at their legacy, virtues and great effects on this nation, that fact [that individual women are not always inferior to men] is something that is witnessed in itself.

What Umm Salamah narrated also indicates [the rights of the husband upon her]. She stated that the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) said,

Gob) os ol Ute GAG GHG an

' Number 4425, Book of Military Campaigns, Chapter on the Prophet (peace be upon him) writing to the Emperors of Persia and Rome, and also #7099, The Book on Jrials, Chapter on the trial that will emerge like waves on an ocean.

The Figh of Marriage

“Any woman who dies with her husband being pleased with her shall enter Paradise.”!

The most obvious of the rights of the husband upon his wife are the following:

The evidence for this is in what Husain ibn Muhsin narrated: My aunt narrated to me that she said, “I came to the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) and he said, ‘Are you married?’ I said, ‘Yes.’ He said, ‘How are you with respect to him?’ She said, ‘I do not fail him ina anything except for what I am not able to do.’ He said, ‘See how you are with respect to him for he is your Paradise or your Hell-fire,’”?

The meaning is, “Are you loving towards him, responding to his call or distancing yourself from him, ungrateful with respect to his goodness and favors towards you. You should be very careful for he is the reason for your entering Paradise if he is pleased with you or your entering the Fire if he is displeased with you.”3

And Abu Hurairah narrated that the Messenger of Allah (peace be upon him) said,

ool gt Clo Ye Cll Lene ean y Yank ah ele 3) esl aI Gi gf

See Faidh al-Qadeer, vol. 3, p. 60, by al-Munaawi.

‘The Figh of Marriage

“Tf the woman prayed her five daily prayers, guarded her chastity and obeyed her husband, she will enter Paradise through any gate she wills.”!

However, the obedience by the wife to her husband is only with respect to those things that are good and acceptable. If he orders her to do something forbidden or to fail to perform an obligatory act, she must refuse to obey him. The Prophet (peace be upon him) has said,

Syd) bella LS al Lane (3 WELL Y “There is no obeisance in what is an act of disobedience to Allah.
content_id:
Sef dist iby 36 etal asso OUT yy

' Dicatat oleh Abu Dawood (#345, 346 dan 349), Kitab tentang Kesucian, Bab tentang

ghus/ untuk hari Jumat; al-Tirmidhi (#496), Kitab tentang Shalat, Bab tentang apa yang

diriwayatkan mengenai keutamaan melakukan ghus/ pada hari Jumat. Ibn Khuzaimah (jil. 3, hal. 28) dan ibn Hibbaan (#559) telah menyatakan hadith ini sahih.

[Perlu dicatat bahwa ini adalah salah satu (dan mungkin bukan yang terkuat) penafsiran dari hadith ini. Untuk pembahasan lebih rinci mengenai makna hadith ini, lihat terjemahan penerjemah ini dalam The Friday Prayer Part I: The Fiqh (Ann Arbor, MI: IANA, 1998, edisi ke-2), hal. 6465.]

Fiqh Pernikahan

"Jika salah seorang dari kalian telah lama pergi, maka janganlah ia pulang — secara tiba-tiba! — kepada istrinya di malam hari."2 Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) juga bersabda, feat a wg we fk ato te TL of ota ee Hpi AEG i yb Ul BS be SUT a 1,

"Jika salah seorang dari kalian pulang di malam hari, janganlah ia mendatangi istrinya secara tiba-tiba sehingga wanita yang suaminya telah lama pergi itu sempat mencukur bulu kemaluannya dan membersihkan kotoran dari rambutnya dengan menyisirnya."3

Jaabir juga berkata, "Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) melarang seorang laki-laki pulang mendadak di malam hari kepada istrinya, sehingga mendapati sesuatu yang menimbulkan kecurigaannya terhadap istri-istri atau menjumpai sebagian aib mereka."

Ibn Hajr menjelaskan hadith di atas, menyatakan dalam komentarnya,

Orang yang tiba-tiba mendatangi keluarganya setelah lama tidak ada tanpa mereka sadari, biasanya akan mendapati hal-hal yang tidak menyenangkan. Ia mungkin mendapati istrinya tidak bersiap-siap dalam hal kebersihan dan berhias sebagaimana yang diharapkan dari seorang wanita, dan hal ini dapat menimbulkan kerenggangan di antara mereka. Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) mengisyaratkan aspek tersebut ketika beliau bersabda, "[hingga ia] sempat mencukur bulu kemaluannya dan membersihkan kotoran dari rambutnya dengan menyisirnya." Dari situ, ia mungkin tidak suka berhubungan dengannya saat istri dalam keadaan demikian di mana

' Ungkapan yang digunakan di sini berarti pulang dari perjalanan di malam hari atau di waktu lain secara tiba-tiba. Lihat Fath al-Baari, jil. 9, hal. 340.

Dicatat oleh al-Bukhari (#5244), Kitab tentang Pernikahan, Bab tentang larangan mendatangi istri secara tiba-tiba di malam hari jika seseorang telah lama pergi, agar tidak mendapati sesuatu yang menimbulkan kecurigaannya terhadap istri atau menjumpai sebagian aib mereka.

Dicatat oleh Muslim (#715) [184], Kitab tentang Kepemimpinan Pemerintahan, Bab tentang ketidaksetujuan pulang secara tiba-tiba di malam hari bagi orang yang kembali dari

Dicatat oleh Muslim (#715) [57], Bab tentang Kepemimpinan Pemerintahan, Bab tentang ketidaksetujuan pulang secara tiba-tiba di malam hari.

Fiqh Pernikahan

ia belum membersihkan diri, sehingga ia tidak mendatanginya dalam keadaan yang membuatnya tidak menyukainya. Atau ia mungkin mendapatinya dalam keadaan yang tidak menyenangkan. Syariat berusaha menutupi aib orang. Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) mengisyaratkan aspek ini ketika beliau bersabda, "mendapati sesuatu yang menimbulkan kecurigaannya terhadap istri atau menjumpai sebagian aib mereka." Berdasarkan hal itu, meskipun demikian, jika seseorang memberitahu istrinya tentang waktu kedatangannya dan bahwa ia akan tiba pada waktu tertentu, maka larangan [pulang di malam hari] ini tidak berlaku baginya.1

Istri harus berusaha menjadikan dirinya menarik bagi suaminya dengan berhias untuknya dan membuat dirinya tampak memikat, agar suami tidak berpaling darinya. Salah satu istri Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) berkata kepada beliau, "Wahai Rasulullah, jika seorang wanita tidak berhias untuk suaminya, ia tidak akan berarti baginya."2 Bagian terakhir ini bermakna ia akan menjadi beban baginya dan ia tidak akan suka memandangnya.

Telah diriwayatkan secara sahih bahwa Aishah berhias untuk Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam). Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) pernah mendatanginya dan ia memakai dua gelang perak. Beliau berkata, "Apa ini, wahai Aishah?" Ia menjawab, "Aku membuatnya untuk [kupakai agar] berhias untukmu, wahai Rasulullah."3

1 Lihat Fath al-Baari, jil. 9, hal. 340.

2 Dicatat oleh al-Nasaai (jil. 9, hal. 159), Kitab tentang Berhias, Bab tentang larangan wanita memperlihatkan perhiasan dan emasnya; Ahmad dalam al-Musnad (jil. 2, hal. 440), dari hadith Abu Hurairah. [Hadith tertentu ini telah dinilai dha'if oleh al-Albaani dan al-Arnaoot, dan lain-lain. Lihat Muhammad Naasir al-Deen al-Albaani, Dha'eef Sunan al-Nasaai (Beirut: al-Maktab al-Islaami, 1990), hal. 228; catatan kaki Shuaib al-Arnaaot dan Aadil Murshid pada Ahmad ibn Hanbal, Musnad al-Imaam Ahmad ibn Hanbal (Beirut: Muassasah al-Risaalah, 1997), jil. 15, hal. 423-

Fiqh Pernikahan

Bab Ketujuh Hak-Hak Pasangan Suami Istri

Setelah dikaji, kita mendapati bahwa hak-hak pasangan suami istri terbagi dalam tiga kategori: hak suami, hak istri, dan hak bersama kedua pasangan. Masing-masing akan dijelaskan secara terpisah di bawah ini.

Hak Suami atas Istrinya

Landasan yang menjadi dasar hak-hak suami atas istrinya adalah firman Allah dalam al-Quran,

Lan ih ob iia 35 abi (ead Vay sind oe ols Ne his vay ial obi wat oostass oot \ asl grtatl re Ohl aan Ain sea uy a

"Laki-laki adalah pemimpin bagi perempuan dengan [hak] apa yang Allah lebihkan sebagian mereka atas sebagian yang lain dan dengan apa yang mereka nafkahkan dari harta mereka. Maka perempuan-perempuan yang salihah adalah yang taat [kepada Allah dan suami] dan menjaga diri ketika [suami] tidak ada karena Allah telah menjaga [mereka]. Perempuan-perempuan yang kamu khawatirkan akan nusyuz, [pertama] nasihatilah mereka; [kemudian jika mereka tetap bersikap demikian] tinggalkanlah mereka di tempat tidur; dan [akhirnya] pukullah mereka [dengan pukulan ringan]. Tetapi jika mereka menaatimu [kembali], maka janganlah kamu mencari-cari alasan untuk menyusahkan mereka" (an-Nisaa: 34).

Imam Abu al-Fidaa Ismaeel ibn Katheer menyatakan dalam tafsirnya terhadap firman, "Laki-laki adalah pemimpin bagi perempuan":

Fiqh Pernikahan

Laki-laki adalah pemimpin bagi perempuan "dengan [hak] apa yang Allah lebihkan sebagian mereka atas sebagian yang lain." Dengan kata lain, [secara umum] laki-laki lebih unggul dari perempuan dan laki-laki [secara umum] lebih baik dari perempuan. Inilah mengapa kenabian hanya diberikan kepada laki-laki. Hal yang sama berlaku untuk kepemimpinan pemerintahan tertinggi, sebagaimana Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) bersabda, "Suatu kaum tidak akan beruntung jika urusan mereka [dikendalikan] oleh seorang wanita." Dicatat oleh al-Bukhari.1 Hal yang sama berlaku untuk kehakiman dan jabatan-jabatan lainnya. [Mereka juga memimpin perempuan] karena "apa yang mereka nafkahkan dari harta mereka," yaitu mahar dan nafkah yang Allah wajibkan atas mereka dalam Kitab-Nya yang Mulia dan dalam sunnah Rasul-Nya (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam). Laki-laki lebih baik dari perempuan dalam dirinya sendiri dan ia memiliki keutamaan atas perempuan dan keunggulan. Maka sangat tepatlah bahwa ia menjadi pemimpinnya.2

Ini tidak berarti bahwa hal yang sama berlaku bagi setiap individu. Keunggulan satu kelompok makhluk atas kelompok lain tidak berarti bahwa setiap individu dari kelompok tersebut unggul atas kelompok lain. Oleh karena itu, tidaklah mustahil bahwa sebagian wanita jauh lebih unggul dari sebagian laki-laki. Melihat warisan, keutamaan, dan pengaruh besar mereka terhadap umat ini, kenyataan itu [bahwa wanita secara individual tidak selalu lebih rendah dari laki-laki] adalah sesuatu yang nyata dengan sendirinya.

Apa yang diriwayatkan oleh Umm Salamah juga mengisyaratkan [hak suami atas istri]. Ia menyatakan bahwa Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) bersabda,

Gob) os ol Ute GAG GHG an

1 Nomor 4425, Kitab tentang Peperangan, Bab tentang Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) menulis kepada Kaisar Persia dan Romawi, dan juga #7099, Kitab tentang Fitnah, Bab tentang fitnah yang akan muncul seperti gelombang lautan.

Fiqh Pernikahan

"Wanita mana pun yang meninggal dunia sedangkan suaminya ridha kepadanya, ia akan masuk surga."1

Yang paling nyata dari hak-hak suami atas istrinya adalah sebagai berikut:

Dalilnya adalah apa yang diriwayatkan oleh Husain ibn Muhsin: Bibiku meriwayatkan kepadaku bahwa ia berkata, "Aku datang kepada Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) dan beliau bersabda, 'Apakah kamu sudah menikah?' Aku berkata, 'Ya.' Beliau berkata, 'Bagaimana keadaanmu terhadapnya?' Ia berkata, 'Aku tidak pernah lalai dalam hal apapun kecuali apa yang tidak mampu aku lakukan.' Beliau bersabda, 'Perhatikanlah bagaimana keadaanmu terhadapnya, karena dialah surga atau neraka bagimu.'"2

Maknanya adalah, "Apakah kamu mencintainya, menyambut panggilannya, atau menjauhinya, tidak bersyukur atas kebaikan dan jasa-jasanya kepadamu. Kamu harus sangat berhati-hati karena dialah penyebab kamu masuk surga jika ia ridha kepadamu atau masuk neraka jika ia murka kepadamu."3

Dan Abu Hurairah meriwayatkan bahwa Rasulullah (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) bersabda,

ool gt Clo Ye Cll Lene ean y Yank ah ele 3) esl aI Gi gf

Lihat Faidh al-Qadeer, jilid 3, hlm. 60, oleh al-Munaawi.

Fiqh Pernikahan

"Jika seorang wanita mengerjakan salah lima waktu, menjaga kesucian dirinya, dan menaati suaminya, maka ia akan masuk surga melalui pintu mana saja yang ia kehendaki."

Namun, ketaatan istri kepada suaminya hanya berlaku dalam perkara-perkara yang baik dan ma'ruf. Apabila suami memerintahkannya untuk melakukan sesuatu yang dilarang atau meninggalkan suatu kewajiban, maka ia wajib menolak untuk menaatinya. Nabi (shallallahu 'alaihi wa sallam) telah bersabda,

Syd) bella LS al Lane (3 WELL Y "Tidak ada ketaatan dalam perkara yang merupakan kemaksiatan kepada Allah."

[It should be noted that this is one (and perhaps not the Strongest) interpretation of this الحديث. For a more detailed discussion of the meaning of this الحديث, see this translator’s The Friday Prayer Part I: The Figh (Ann Arbor, MI: IANA, 1998, 2 edition), pp. 6465.2]

The Figh of Marriage

“If one of you is gone for a long time, he should not return - unexpectedly! to his wife at night.”? النبي ﷺ (صلى الله عليه وسلم) also said, feat a wg we fk ato te TL of ota ee Hpi AEG i yb Ul BS be SUT a 1,

“Tf one of you returns at night, he should not come to his wife unexpectedly until the one whose husband has been gone can remove her pubic hairs and clean the filth from her hair by combing it.”3

Jaabir also said, “رسول الله ﷺ (صلى الله عليه وسلم) forbade that a man returns expectedly at night to his wife, finding something that will arouse his suspicion concerning the wives or coming across some of their faults,”

Tbn Hajr explained the above الحديث, stating in his commentary,

The one who rushes upon his household [unexpectedly] after a long absence without them realizing it will usually come across some displeasing aspects. ' ‘The phrase used here means to return from a journey at night or at any other time ynexpectedly. See Fath al-Baari, vol. 9, B 340.

Recorded by al-Bukhari (#5244), Book on Marriage, Chapter on not comit unexpectedly to one’s wife at night if one has been gone long, lest he may fin something that will arouse his suspicion concerning the wives or he may come qcross some of their faults.

Recorded by Muslim (#715) [184], Book on Governmental Authority, Chapter on the disapproval of returning unexpectedly at night for the one who returns from a

Rav ded by Muslim (#715) [57], Chapter on Governmental Authority, Chapter on the disapproval of returning unexpectedly at night.

The Figh of Marriage

she has not cleaned herself and so he does not come upon her while she is in a state that will cause him to dislike her. Or he may come across her while she is an unpleasant state. The law tries to conceal people’s faults. النبي ﷺ (صلى الله عليه وسلم) alluded to this aspect when he said, “finding something that will arouse his suspicion concerning the wives or coming across some of their faults.” Based on that, though, if someone informs his wife about when he is going to arrive and that he should be coming at such and such time, this prohibition [of coming at night] does not apply to him.!

The wife must try to make herself appealing to her husband by beautifying herself for him and making herself attractive, in order for him not to shun her. One of the wives of النبي ﷺ (صلى الله عليه وسلم) said to him, “O رسول الله ﷺ, if a woman does not beautify herself for her husband, she will not be important to him.”? The last part means she will become a burden upon him and he will not like to look at her.

It is authentically reported that Aishah would beautify herself for النبي ﷺ (peace be upoa him), النبي ﷺ (صلى الله عليه وسلم) came upon her once and she had two silver rings. He said, “What is this, O Aishah?” She replied, “I made them in order [to wear them] to beautify myself for you, O رسول الله ﷺ.”3

| See Fath al-Baari, vol. 9, p. 340.

? Recorded by al-Nasaai bol 9, p. 159), Book on Beautification, Chapter on the disapproval of women Showing their jewelry and gold; Ahmad in a/-Musnad (vol. 2, p. 440), from the الحديث of Abu Hurairah. [This particular الحديث has been graded weak by both al-Albaani and al-Arnaoot, et al. See Muhammad Naasir al-Deen alAlbaani, Dhaeef Sunan al-Nasaai (Beirut: al-Maktab al-Islaami, 1990), p. 228; Shuaib al-Arnaaot and Aadil Murshid’s footnotes to Ahmad ibn Hanbal, Musnad alImaam {mad ibn Hanbal (Beirut: Muassasah al-Risaalah, 1997), vol. 15, pp. 423-

The Figh of Marriage

Chapter Seven The Rights of Spouses

Upon examination, we find that the rights of the spouses fall into three categories: the rights of the husband, the rights of the wife and common rights of both spouses. Each shall be explained separately below.

The Rights of the Husband upon His Wife

The foundation upon which the husband’s rights over his wife are based is the verse in the القرآن الكريم,

Lan ih ob iia 35 abi (ead Vay sind oe ols Ne his vay ial obi wat oostass oot \ asl grtatl re Ohl aan Ain sea uy a

“Men are in charge of women by [right of] what [qualities] الله has given one over the other and what they spend [in support] from their wealth. So righteous women are devoutly obedient, guarding in [the husband’s] absence what الله would have them guard. But those [women] from whom you fear defiance, [first] admonish them; [then if they persist] forsake. them in their beds and [finally] strike them [lightly]. But if they obey you [once more], seek no means against them” (al-Nisaa 34).

Imam Abu al-Fidaa Ismaeel ibn Katheer stated in his commentary to the words, “Men are in charge of women”:

‘The Fight of Marriage

Men are in charge of women “by [right of] what [qualities] الله has given one over the other.” In other words, [in general} men are superior to women and men are [in general] better than women. This is why prophethood was only given to men. The same is true for the supreme governmental head, as النبي ﷺ (صلى الله عليه وسلم) said, “A people will not prosper whose affairs are [at the controls of] a woman.” Recorded by alAl-Bukhari! The same is true for judgeship and other Positions. [They are also in charge of women] due to “what they spend [in support] from their wealth,” that is, the dower and maintenance that الله has made obligatory upon them in His Noble Book and in the السنة النبوية of His Messenger (صلى الله عليه وسلم). Man is better than woman in himself and he has preference over her and superiority. Hence, it is most appropriate that he be in charge of her.?

This does not mean that the same is true for each individual. The superiority of one class of beings over another does not mean that each individual of that class is superior to the other class. Therefore, it is not impossible that some women are greatly superior to some men. Looking at their legacy, virtues and great effects on this nation, that fact [that individual women are not always inferior to men] is something that is witnessed in itself.

What Umm Salamah narrated also indicates [the rights of the husband upon her]. She stated that رسول الله ﷺ (صلى الله عليه وسلم) said,

Gob) os ol Ute GAG GHG an

' Number 4425, Book of Military Campaigns, Chapter on النبي ﷺ (صلى الله عليه وسلم) writing to the Emperors of Persia and Rome, and also #7099, The Book on Jrials, Chapter on the trial that will emerge like waves on an ocean.

The Figh of Marriage

“Any woman who dies with her husband being pleased with her shall enter Paradise.”!

The most obvious of the rights of the husband upon his wife are the following:

The evidence for this is in what Husain ibn Muhsin narrated: My aunt narrated to me that she said, “I came to رسول الله ﷺ (صلى الله عليه وسلم) and he said, ‘Are you married?’ I said, ‘Yes.’ He said, ‘How are you with respect to him?’ She said, ‘I do not fail him ina anything except for what I am not able to do.’ He said, ‘See how you are with respect to him for he is your Paradise or your Hell-fire,’”?

The meaning is, “Are you loving towards him, responding to his call or distancing yourself from him, ungrateful with respect to his goodness and favors towards you. You should be very careful for he is the reason for your entering Paradise if he is pleased with you or your entering the Fire if he is displeased with you.”3

And Abu Hurairah narrated that رسول الله ﷺ (صلى الله عليه وسلم) said,

ool gt Clo Ye Cll Lene ean y Yank ah ele 3) esl aI Gi gf

See Faidh al-Qadeer, vol. 3, p. 60, by al-Munaawi.

‘The Figh of Marriage

“Tf the woman prayed her five daily prayers, guarded her chastity and obeyed her husband, she will enter Paradise through any gate she wills.”!

However, the obedience by the wife to her husband is only with respect to those things that are good and acceptable. If he orders her to do something forbidden or to fail to perform an obligatory act, she must refuse to obey him. النبي ﷺ (صلى الله عليه وسلم) has said,

Syd) bella LS al Lane (3 WELL Y “There is no obeisance in what is an act of disobedience to الله.
